glossaryIntensity: How this week's training time splits across low (easy, Z1–Z2), moderate (around threshold, Z3) and high (Z4 and above) intensity. Polarized = lots of easy plus some hard; pyramidal = decreasing from easy to hard; threshold = concentrated around Z3.
glossaryDecoupling: Pw:Hr decoupling — how much your power-to-heart-rate efficiency dropped in the second half of the ride. Under ~5% is good aerobic durability; higher suggests cardiac drift from fatigue, heat or a weaker aerobic base.
powerZonesNoFtp: Set your FTP in your profile to see power-zone distribution.
glossaryCtl: Chronic Training Load — a 42-day weighted average of your daily training stress. A proxy for fitness: it rises slowly as you train consistently.
glossaryAtl: Acute Training Load — a 7-day weighted average of daily training stress. A proxy for fatigue: it reacts quickly to recent hard sessions.
glossaryTsb: Training Stress Balance (CTL − ATL), also called Form. Positive means fresh and ready; strongly negative means fatigued.
glossaryTss: Training Stress Score — the overall load of a session, combining intensity and duration. ~100 ≈ one hard hour at threshold.
glossaryHrss: Heart Rate Stress Score — a TSS-equivalent computed from heart rate when power isn't available.
glossaryIf: Intensity Factor — Normalized Power divided by your FTP. How hard the session was relative to threshold (1.0 = at FTP).
glossaryNp: Normalized Power — an estimate of the steady power that would have cost the same effort as your variable ride. Always ≥ average power.
glossaryVi: Variability Index — Normalized Power ÷ average power. Near 1.0 means a steady ride; above ~1.1 means surgy, stop-and-go efforts.
tsbRecommendationFresh: Freshness in range. Good window for intense sessions or racing.
tsbRecommendationTransition: Transition zone. You can train moderately.
trendHighAtl: High ATL = Recent hard training or racing
trendPositiveTsb: Positive TSB = Fresh and ready to perform
